brief.subject: Mathematics
brief.learningObjective: Recognise equivalent fractions using visual and numerical representations.
brief.studentAction: Choose the equivalent fraction in three short questions and read immediate explanatory feedback.

brief.subject: Science
brief.learningObjective: Distinguish balanced and unbalanced forces in familiar situations.
brief.studentAction: Classify four situations, then use the feedback to explain the resultant force.

brief.subject: Humanities
brief.learningObjective: Evaluate the reliability of a historical source using provenance and purpose.
brief.studentAction: Judge two short source descriptions and justify one answer before checking feedback.

brief.subject: English
brief.learningObjective: Identify persuasive language choices and explain their intended effect.
brief.studentAction: Choose the most persuasive sentence for each audience and compare the feedback.

brief.subject: English
brief.learningObjective: Arrange topic, supporting and concluding sentences into a coherent paragraph.
brief.studentAction: Put five sentences in a logical order and check the explanation.

brief.subject: Humanities
brief.learningObjective: Sequence key milestones in Singapore's path to internal self-government.
brief.studentAction: Arrange five dated events, then check the chronological explanation.
brief.content: Use the years 1948, 1955, 1957, 1958 and 1959 without naming any living pupils.

brief.subject: Languages
brief.learningObjective: Match common Malay classroom words to their English meanings.
brief.studentAction: Match six Malay words to meanings and use feedback to practise any errors.
brief.content: Include buku, meja, kerusi, pensel, papan putih and beg.

brief.subject: Mathematics
brief.learningObjective: Relate the gradient and intercept in y = mx + c to the graph of a straight line.
brief.studentAction: Adjust m and c with sliders, observe the graph and answer a prediction question.

brief.subject: Science
brief.learningObjective: Relate launch speed and angle to the horizontal range of a projectile.
brief.studentAction: Predict, adjust speed and angle, then compare the plotted trajectory with the prediction.
brief.content: Use Earth gravity and safe numeric expression trees only.

brief.subject: Mathematics
brief.learningObjective: Explore how rectangle dimensions change while perimeter remains fixed.
brief.studentAction: Move a width slider, inspect the calculated length and area, then answer what maximises area.

brief.subject: Science
brief.learningObjective: Explain evaporation, condensation and precipitation in the water cycle.
brief.studentAction: Complete a three-stage retrieval check and read process-focused feedback.

brief.subject: Humanities
brief.learningObjective: Compare stakeholder perspectives when allocating a limited community budget.
brief.studentAction: Make three choices in a branching scenario and examine the trade-offs in the feedback.

brief.subject: Mathematics
brief.studentAction: Sort eight ratio statements into two groups and check each misconception.
